```plaintext
Indirect Tax laws
Portionwise Test - 3. [Customs & FTP]

Q1 Import - 12/1/19
    Put to use - 1/2/19
    Clearance for home consumption - 5/4/22

    Concessional Rate - 5%
    Normal Rate - 20%

Computation of Custom duty payable
Particulars                                                                 ₹
Amount of Duty to be paid on clearance.

Value of machine as on 5/4/22
Original Value of Machine  20,00,000
Depreciation on value of machine
2019  1st yr. - 4 Quarter - 4% X 4 = 16%
2020  2nd yr. - 4 Quarter - 3% X 4 = 12%
2021  3rd yr. - 4 Quarter - 3% X 4 = 12%
2022  4th yr. - 2 Quarter - 2.5% X 4 = 10%
                                                      50%

∴ Value of Machine = 20 lakh - 50% = 10 lakh
∴ Amount of Duty payable
   = 10,00,000 X 20%                                      200000

(-) Amount of Duty Actually paid
      20,00,000 X 5%                                      (100000)

∴ Duty payable                                              100000
```

**Q2. Anti Dumping Duty**

- Such duty is imposed when goods are dumped at reduced prices causing injury to domestic market.
- Such duty can be imposed retrospectively only when following conditions are satisfied.
  ➤ There is history of such dumping
  ➤ Such dumping has caused major serious injury overtime.

Hence in given scenario Chaintop Industries has to prove the above conditions to make antidumping duty liable on same retrospectively.

**Q3. Computation of Custom Duty Payable**

| Particulars | ₹ |
|-------------|---|
| Assessable Value of Sodium Nitrate | 30,00,000 |
| Basic Custom duty @10% | 3,00,000 |
| Social Welfare Surcharge @10% | 30,000 |
| Safeguard duty @30% | 9,00,000 |
| ➤ Total value incl. Custom duty | 42,30,000 |
| IGST @ 12% | 5,07,600 |
| ➤ Total Custom duty | 12,30,000 |
| ➤ Total custom duty + IGST payable | 17,37,600 |

**Note**

- Safeguard duty is not imposed when goods are imported from developing countries if goods imported from developing countries < 8% of total imports of such goods.
- In given case & since the share of import of Sodium Nitrate against total imports from developing country is 4%, safeguard duty shall be levied.

Q4. Project Import

- Importer when starting new plant or wants to expand its business, it requires various machinery, instruments & apparatus.
- These goods have are different & has different rate of duty.
- If the person imports the same, he shall be liable to pay heavy custom duty making project inviable.
- In such case Project Import comes into picture wherein goods are imported at concessional rate for the entire project.

Q5. Computation of Assessable Value

Particulars

Cost of Machine (ex-factory)  
(+) Transport charges to port for shipment  
(+) Handling charges for loading machine  
(+) Design charges and engineering work  
Total Free on Board (FOB)  

FOB in ₹  
(+) Freight ($2000 x 70)  
(+) Insurance (1827000 x 1.125%)  
Assessable Value (CIF) [rounded off]

Notes:

- Buying commission do not form part of Assessable Value
- Value up until port of importation are the ones that are considered in determining assessable value therefore
- Unloading & handling charges paid at place of importation & transport charges from Mumbai to Cochin port are ignored
- Since Insurance charges are unascertained, it is computed at 1.125% of FOB.

$  
20000  
1000  
100  
5000  
26100  

₹  
1827000  
140000  
20553.75  
1987554

Q6.

- Bill of entry shall be filed by end of the day preceding the date of arrival of vessel/aircraft, but not before 30 days from the date of expected arrival.
- Payment of duty can be deferred by following class of persons, however the condition for filing Bill of entry for them remains the same viz. namely
  - Authorised Economic Operator (AEO) Tier II & Tier III
  - Authorised Public Undertaking (APU)
- Hence penalty can be levied on late filing of Bill of Entry as the same has to be filed in advance.

Q7.

(1) Laxmi Company can pay additional provisional duty if there is no bar on extra payment of duty than so assessed.

(2) Computation of Amount of Interest Payable

Particulars ₹
Additional Amount Payable
Amt paid on 15/12/23 - 200000
Amt voluntary paid on - 150000
20/1/24
Total Amount paid 350000
Amount actually payable
upon final assessment 400000
∴ Additional Amount Payable 50000

Interest Payable
Additional Amt = 50000 X 62 X 15% 1274
365
On voluntary Amt = 150000 X 31 X 15% 1911
365
53185

Q8. 
- Since the order for home consumption is passed & relevant duty at such time was already paid, therefore importer is not liable for any differential duty.
- Rate of duty even as on date of filing BOE for home consumption or arrival of vessel/aircraft whichever is later shall apply.
- Just because in given case BL Ltd. does not made application to store such goods in warehouse until actual use/sale does not account for non clearance.

Q9. 
- If goods are found defective or not as per specification importer has respective options:—
1) Re-export
   In such case refund of import duty shall be considered from date of re-export.
2) Relinquishment of Title
   Herein refund of import duty shall be from date of relinquishment.
3) Destroy
   Date of destruction shall be considered as date for granting refund of import duty.
